program.name: Cerritos Recycled Water Program
program.description: Since 1988, Cerritos has operated one of the region's most successful recycled water programs, distributing treated water through 25 miles of dedicated purple pipe for landscape irrigation across 200+ acres of parks, medians, and green spaces.

infrastructure.capitalCostNote: Estimated based on regional recycled water infrastructure costs per mile (~$356K/mile for 25 miles of distribution). Actual Cerritos capital costs are not publicly itemized.
infrastructure.maintenanceNote: Estimated at ~5% of capital cost annually, consistent with water utility maintenance benchmarks.

waterRates.potableSource: MWD Tier 1 wholesale treated rate (~$6.50 per 1,000 gallons)
waterRates.recycledSource: Typical Southern California recycled water rate (~$3.00 per 1,000 gallons)
waterRates.rateDisclaimer: Rates are regional averages for comparison purposes. Actual rates negotiated by Cerritos may differ.

scenarios.name: Base Case
scenarios.description: Current operations at today's water rates

scenarios.name: Drought Scenario
scenarios.description: Potable water costs increase 50% during severe drought

scenarios.name: Expansion Scenario
scenarios.description: 25% capacity increase with additional distribution lines

equivalences.note: Olympic pool = 660,000 gal (FINA). Avg CA household water use ~55,000 gal/year (DWR).

sources.name: City of Cerritos — Recycled Water Program
